title: Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae
intro: Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae, commonly known as New Zealand kowhai, is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for respiratory issues, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae is classified within the family Capparaceae. It belongs to the order Brassicales. The species Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae is native to New Zealand and is often found in coastal areas and sandy dunes. It is a shrub or small tree that grows to a height of around 3-6 meters. The classification of Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae is further supported by its anatomical and morphological characteristics.
